content.text: The mailbox driver should support both internal and external mailbox features.

The external mailbox feature is used to exchange data between the main domain and SAIL. When the data is received from main domain, the corresponding mailbox channel notification function is called. For each mailbox channel, the notification function must be configured using the config parameter

Internal mailbox feature is used to exchange the data between the SAIL cores. When the data is received from any core, the corresponding mailbox channel notification function is called. For each mailbox channel, the notification function must be configured using the config parameter

rows: MailboxName
Mailbox region name
we can give any meaningful name
MailboxAddress
This parameter configures the system memory address where the mailbox must be installed
![warning](https://inside-docupedia.bosch.com/confluence/plugins/servlet/twitterEmojiRedirector?id=26a0) Need to check how to find this region
Maximum no of Sub region
Configures the maximum item number for the mailbox sub region
Mailbox priority
Priority for the Mailbox region Range: 0 to 255
Mailbox Size
Maximum size of the mailbox Default value:1024 Range: 0 to 2097152
Mailbox type
Type of the mailbox; internal or external Default value: MAILBOX_EXTERNAL Range: MAILBOX_EXTERNAL: mailbox type is external MAILBOX_INTERNAL: mailbox type is internal
Mailbox Sub region name
Mailbox sub region name
we can give any meaningful name
Mailbox IPCC Channel ref
Map the corresponding the IPCC channel configured in CDD_IPCC configuration
IPCC channel ID that maps to the corresponding mailbox channel
Mailbox sub region callback
Configures the callback function that must be called when the data is received on the mailbox sub region
Mailbox sub region Item number max
Configures the maximum item number for the mailbox sub region.
Mailbox sub region Item size max
Configures the maximum size of the mailbox sub region.
Mailbox sub region name
Name of the subregion
/dev/sail/log ![warning](https://inside-docupedia.bosch.com/confluence/plugins/servlet/twitterEmojiRedirector?id=26a0) Not sure about the use of this configuration parameter
